collection_failures: do215 (partial — June 22, 25, 26, 28 not covered; URL retrieval failed in scheduled-task context)
songkick (partial — full June page too large to process; 2 events confirmed via supplemental search)

top3.title: NFC Sculpture Workshop
top3.why: Build small clay sculptures with NFC tags embedded inside that trigger shortcuts on your phone — half art project, half hardware hack, at a DIY electronics bookstore with a no-one-turned-away-for-lack-of-funds door policy. Iffy Books keeps the barrier floor as low as it gets: $4.50 for the kit, pay what you can for instruction. If you've been wanting to start embedding tech into physical objects, this is the room to start in Philly.

top3.title: Gothic night: Die Sexual, Ronnie Stone & DJ Baby Berlin
top3.why: Die Sexual bring a heavy, bass-driven gothic rock sound that rarely makes it to Philly — this is a proper goth night with live bands, not a DJ bar set. Doors at 7, show at 8, $15 advance at a venue that keeps the room the right size for this kind of music. 21+.

top3.title: CONTRACHARGE (chi), AGONESIAC, DISCLAIM
top3.why: CONTRACHARGE travel from Chicago — shows like this at Cousin Danny's are exactly why the DIY network exists. The early 6 PM start means you can make this and still catch something later. Cost and address not confirmed in source; check Ask A Punk or reach out before going.

events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Bourse
events.note: Kane Parsons directs the internet horror meme turned film — a strange doorway opens in a furniture showroom basement. Multiple showtimes: 1pm, 3:50pm, 6:30pm, 9pm.

events.title: THE FURIOUS (2025) — PFS SpringFest Selection
events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society East
events.note: Explosive martial arts film with Xie Miao and Joe Taslim. SpringFest 2026 selection. Multiple showtimes: 1pm, 4pm, 6:45pm, 9:30pm.

events.title: I LOVE BOOSTERS (2025)
events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Bourse
events.note: Boots Riley (Sorry to Bother You) directs a caper comedy about professional shoplifters targeting a fashion maven. Multiple showtimes: 1:15pm, 4pm, 6:40pm, 9:15pm.

events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Center
events.note: Sapphic coming-of-age drama. Part of PFS's Pride Month curated series.

events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Center
events.note: Audrey Hepburn and Shirley MacLaine in William Wyler's searing drama about slander. Part of PFS's Pride Month curated series.

events.note: Noel Marshall's insane big-cat film with Tippi Hedren and Melanie Griffith. Part of the PFS After Hours Curated series.

events.note: Local filmmakers of all ages and backgrounds. Q&A with filmmakers follows.

events.note: SOLD OUT. Filmmaker Ruth Leitman in attendance, Q&A moderated by Jeff Krulik (Heavy Metal Parking Lot). An encore screening is available Tuesday June 23.

events.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Center
events.note: Paul Verhoeven's cult classic. PFS After Hours Curated series.

events.title: West Philly Sanctuary: Beaded Keychain-Making Workshop (World Cup Edition)
events.note: West Philly Sanctuary (immigrant support org) hosts a free World Cup–themed keychain workshop. All ages, alcohol-free venue. 12–2 PM.

events.title: community night: bring your own craft + queer book swap
events.note: Pride Month community night — bring a craft to work on, bring a queer book to swap. Free at Cartesian Brewing.

events.title: Custom Cutting Philly
events.note: Haircutting workshop focusing on natural texture. Hosted by Caitlin Helle.

events.title: NFC Sculpture Workshop
events.note: Embed NFC tags in Sculpey clay sculptures to trigger phone shortcuts. Kit $4.50 with tax, instructor donation $5–$15, no one turned away for lack of funds.

top3.title: WILDWOOD, NJ (1994) — Encore Screening
top3.why: Ruth Leitman's underground Hi-8 documentary about a 1994 New Jersey beach town sold out its PhilaMOCA premiere on Monday — this encore exists because people couldn't get in. Leitman will be in the room for a Q&A, moderated by Jeff Krulik (director of Heavy Metal Parking Lot). $10 at PhilaMOCA, doors at 7.

top3.title: Black Country, New Road
top3.why: Black Country, New Road are one of the defining acts of current UK experimental music — chamber pop, post-rock, folk, and grief tangled into something constantly evolving. Philly dates are not frequent, and Franklin Music Hall is the right room to feel it. The kind of show that gets talked about afterward.

top3.title: Land to the Tillers Playtest
top3.why: Playtesting a game called Land to the Tillers at an anarchist bookstore is already a sentence worth showing up for — leftist theory as game design, at Wooden Shoe. This is community-built work in progress, and showing up means shaping it while it's still being made. No cost listed.

honorable_mentions.venue: Philadelphia Film Society, Film Society Center

events.title: Black Country, New Road
events.note: UK experimental rock/chamber pop. One of the most acclaimed current acts in the UK — don't sleep.

events.title: Spoon & The Beths: US Tour 2026
events.note: Spoon + The Beths co-headlining at The Fillmore. Large corporate venue, but a solid pairing.

events.note: Terrence Malick's 1978 masterpiece — free as part of Wawa Welcome America. Two showtimes: 4pm and 7pm.

events.title: Open Streets: Midtown Village
events.note: Seven blocks closed to cars every Tuesday 4–9pm through June, with live music, performances, and a family zone.

events.note: Free FIFA World Cup fan event at Lemon Hill. Running through July 4.

events.title: The Writing Room
events.note: Co-working writing session in three 40-minute silent blocks with accountability check-ins. Writers of any kind welcome. BYO coffee, computer, notepads.

events.title: Mural Making Workshop with Chenlin Cai and Xingzi Liang
events.venue: Moore College of Art & Design, Room SP 202
events.note: Hands-on mural painting workshop exploring cultural storytelling through art, with artists Chenlin Cai and Xingzi Liang of Sunny Design & Rainy Art Studio. Hosted by AIGA Philadelphia.

events.title: Vogue Practice Session
events.note: Recurring weekly Vogue Practice Session at the Rotunda. 6–9 PM, all ages, alcohol-free.

top3.title: Speculative Futures Reading Group: 'The Ones Who Walk From Omelas' by Ursula Le Guin
top3.why: Three linked stories — Le Guin's 'The Ones Who Walk From Omelas,' NK Jemisin's direct response 'The Ones Who Stay and Fight,' and Isabel J. Kim's newer riff — make this a living conversation across generations of speculative fiction writers about complicity and collective action. Literary theory meets leftist ethics meets actual fiction at a DIY bookstore with a donation jar. All three texts are available online; come prepared or come to listen.

top3.title: Lip Critic w/ Flatwounds + Bejalvin
top3.why: Lip Critic are a Philly noise-rock band with a cult following for good reason — angular, propulsive, and genuinely weird in the best sense. R5 brings them to PhilaMOCA for an all-ages show; Flatwounds and Bejalvin open. $20, doors 7:30.

top3.title: DoYeon Kim Quartet (Ars Nova Workshop)
top3.why: Korean-born Philadelphia bassist DoYeon Kim brings her quartet to Solar Myth under the Ars Nova Workshop banner — one of the sharpest contemporary jazz and experimental music presenters in the city. If you want to hear what's happening at the edges of jazz in Philadelphia right now, this is the room.

events.title: THE BANSHEE CHAPTER (2013) — Philadelphia Horror Film Night (Online)
events.note: Online watch-along. The Banshee Chapter (2013) — journalist investigates a friend's disappearance linked to a government chemical and a strange radio signal. Via Discord.

events.title: Speculative Futures Reading Group: 'The Ones Who Walk From Omelas' by Ursula Le Guin
events.note: Reading Le Guin alongside NK Jemisin's response and Isabel J. Kim's riff. All three texts free online. July book: Ada Hoffman's Ignore all Previous Instructions.

events.title: Franklin's Key — Pig Iron Theatre Company
events.note: Barrymore Award–winning sci-fi adventure from Pig Iron Theatre. Runs through June 28. Multiple showtimes.

events.title: Analog A/V Session
events.note: Experiment with VHS and cassette tapes — analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog transfers. Equipment provided. Donation $5–$15, no one turned away.

events.title: DJ Basixxx with MIXXX
events.note: Intro to MIXXX, free/open-source DJ software. Bring a laptop and headphones. Optional: separate USB keyboard to use as a DJ controller.

events.title: Weekly Improvised Music Drop-in Jam Session
events.note: Recurring weekly Rotunda drop-in jam. 12–2 PM, all ages, alcohol-free.

top3.title: of Montreal and Cormae
top3.why: of Montreal have been making maximalist, genre-shifting psychedelic pop since the late '90s — glam, prog, funk, and absurdist theater at once — and Philly appearances aren't frequent. Underground Arts is the right room for this kind of show. An event worth clearing your evening for.

top3.title: Prewn
top3.why: Prewn make slow-burning indie rock with emotional weight — the kind of band that rewards a quiet room and full attention. $15 at PhilaMOCA, R5 all-ages, doors 7:30.

top3.title: SWEATFEST @ PENTRIDGE STATION
top3.why: Multi-act DIY fest hitting punk, hardcore, no wave, noise rock, power pop, and post-punk in one session at Pentridge Station. The Ask A Punk listing confirms live music and a 5 PM start — cost and full lineup TBD, check the link.

events.title: Cyber Risk & Resilience in Education in the Age of AI (Online)
events.note: Panel on AI-era cyber threats in K-12 education. Speakers from Microsoft, University of Maryland, and school district CIOs. Online event.

events.note: Thursday Night Art Club — bring your own project, no instruction, just community workspace. Easels and basic materials provided.

top3.title: Beginner Soldering: LED Spinning Top
top3.why: No soldering experience required — you're building a top that flashes LEDs when it spins, and the kit is $5.39. Iffy Books' beginner soldering workshops are legitimately good entry points to electronics work, run by patient instructors with a no-one-turned-away door policy. The cost floor doesn't get lower.

top3.title: Bike Routes w/ Wormy + Innerlove
top3.why: R5 brings Bike Routes — a Chicago indie rock band with road-tested, well-crafted songs — to PhilaMOCA for an all-ages Friday night. $15, doors 7:30. Wormy and Innerlove open; both worth arriving early for.

top3.title: PARC First Aid Training
top3.why: Philly Action Ready Coalition teaches practical street medicine at the anarchist bookstore — Narcan use, CPR, basic scans. No certifications, no jargon, just usable knowledge. Events like this are what mutual aid infrastructure actually looks like in practice.

events.title: Bike Routes w/ Wormy + Innerlove
events.note: R5 Productions. All ages. Doors 7:30.

events.note: Post-punk/punk, out of town band (Positronix). DIY show at Cousin Danny's.

events.note: Free goth night. 21+.

events.title: Supergirl (2026)
events.note: Kara Zor-El teams with an unlikely companion on an interstellar revenge mission. Wide theatrical release.

events.title: Fundraising Market for LAVA Space
events.note: Free market fundraiser supporting LAVA Space. 5–9 PM, all ages, alcohol-free.

events.title: PARC First Aid Training
events.note: Street medicine basics: scans, Narcan, CPR. No certifications. Philly Action Ready Coalition.

events.title: You Da Birthday: Craft Collective Turns One
events.note: One-year anniversary of The Craft Collective — music, open crafting, and community. Open to all.

events.title: Beginner Soldering: LED Spinning Top
events.note: Build an LED spinning top. No experience needed. Kit $5.39, instructor donation $5–$15, notaflof.

top3.title: LAYER MEAT, SPECTRAL FORCES, VOID OF INFINITE CONSEQUENCE: A Benefit Show for Sol Collective
top3.why: Three experimental acts — avant-garde, free jazz, and noise — playing a benefit for Sol Collective, a West Philadelphia community arts and social justice organization. God's Automatic Body is a DIY space most of the city doesn't know about yet; shows there have a specificity that bigger venues lose. This one has community stakes attached and will be talked about.

top3.title: JINSEI — Philly Premiere
top3.why: JINSEI gets its Philadelphia premiere at PhilaMOCA — which is already a meaningful signal about what kind of film this is. $12, doors at 7. Details on the film itself are sparse, which at PhilaMOCA usually means something genuinely strange is in store.

top3.title: Ramageddon
top3.why: Ten local acts in one afternoon and evening at the Rotunda — HECCRA, ULTRA DELUXE, SLOW BURNING DAYDREAM, SODASEAS, POWERLINE TRAIL, GUILLERMO GUILLOTINE, LINDEN, IT HELPS ME SLEEP, ALL ENDS LEAD TO YOU, KWARTER ZIP. $15 advance for the density of local talent alone is a steal. All ages, alcohol-free, the Rotunda at full capacity for a showcase has a specific Philly energy.

events.title: Tarique Connelly — A Teacher's Odyssey
events.note: Philadelphia public school educator presents his memoir on grit, global teaching, and the literacy crisis. Free at the Free Library.

events.title: Seen/Unseen Listening Session
events.note: Audio series by Philadelphian Gigi Guida — a noticing tour through City Hall surfacing power in landscape and language. Facilitated by Gigi Guida and educator Mya Gordon.

events.note: Show off your DIY laptop (SBC + LCD + keyboard in a custom enclosure). LCDs, keyboards, and wooden boxes for sale. Help others get started building.

events.note: DC215 DEF CON group Philadelphia. Casual CTF study session — come to work or just talk security.

events.note: Goth club night. DJ Dr Octo-Pussy. 21+, 9pm–3am. $30+.

events.title: cake picnic
events.note: BYO cake (homemade or store-bought), make decorated cake cards to swap. Hosted by let me know club.

top3.title: Anarchy Summer Reading Group
top3.why: Emma Goldman's Anarchism and Other Essays alongside Kropotkin's The Conquest of Bread — two foundational texts, one anarchist bookstore with a free door. Iffy Books runs this as a biweekly series through the summer; this is the session to start with if you've never read either. Come with questions or just come to listen.

top3.title: The Human League with Soft Cell & Alison Moyet
top3.why: The Human League, Soft Cell, and Alison Moyet on a single bill is a lineup that shouldn't exist outside a festival, and yet here it is at The Met. Three synthpop and new wave acts at the peak of their cultural moment, five decades in — the ticket price reflects it, but so does the occasion. A once-in-years bill.

top3.title: Kirk Franklin — Free Gospel Concert
top3.why: Kirk Franklin performing with a 250-member choir at Independence National Historical Park for free, 7–9 PM, as part of Wawa Welcome America. Gospel and soul at the highest register, outdoors, zero barrier to entry. The full-choir format makes this more spectacle than concert — worth showing up for even if gospel isn't your usual lane.

events.title: The Human League with Soft Cell & Alison Moyet
events.note: Three synthpop/new wave legends. WXPN presented. Large venue but genuinely unmissable lineup.

events.title: Kirk Franklin — Free Gospel Concert
